basics.name: Giacomo Tüfekçi
basics.headline: Full-Stack Developer
basics.summary: I'm a Full-Stack Developer based in Ludwigshafen am Rhein, Germany, with a background in web development, software engineering, and game design. I enjoy building end-to-end solutions and working seamlessly across various programming languages and web frameworks. Beyond just writing code, I value strong project management to ensure ideas actually make it to production efficiently and on time. When I’m not at my keyboard, I’m usually tinkering with electronics, keeping up with new tech, or tending to my garden. I also enjoy winding down with gaming, anime, and web novels. I speak fluent German and English, and I always aim to bring a practical, creative approach to the software I build.

highlights: Development of an Download Manager as a Prototype in PHP and than in Svelte
Development of an Web Application for the Management of a Cleaning Company
Development of an Web Application for the Management of an QR Code based Redirection System
Development of Apps, Plugins, Wrappers and Apis for Deta.space
Development of an Web Application which enables playing retro games in the Browser based on WASM Compiled Emulators.
Development of an Android Application to read Web & Light Novels from different Providers in an unified UI.
Development of an API & Crawler to build an index of Web & Light Novels from different Providers.
Some Prototype ML/AI Applications which can generate Content, Images, Translate etc. which is used in different Projects.
Development of an Network Bridge between Web/Cloud to VisualBasic Application
Development of an Application/API to automate Facebook Events
Development of an APP for the Android and iOS Platform for Room Booking

employments.summary: I submitted my resignation as the project was terminated based on my recommendation. The progress made did not align with the expectations as documented by the management. Although an alternative employment opportunity was offered, I declined.
highlights: Planned and ReDesigned a Cloud/Web based Management System

employments.summary: Responsibilities included developing Web2Print, booking (pmkino) and E-Commerce solutions, as well as planning and implementing websites using WordPress. Promoted to Lead Web Developer / Software Developer after one year.
highlights: Planned, Developed and Managed a booking, website and web2print system for cinemas (pmkino) using PhalconPHP, Wordpress, MongoDB, Python, NodeJS and ReactJS
Developed a Web2Print solution using PhalconPHP, Wordpress, MongoDB, Python, NodeJS and ReactJS
Developed a Wordpress Plugin and Theme environment for Messe Bremen Events, which was used in 10+ Websites.

employments.summary: Transfered from Subsidiary image advertising gmbh & co. kg, started as a Software Developer, I developed Web2Print, database, and E-Commerce solutions while also leading the planning and implementation of websites. Within one year, I was promoted to IT Manager
Implemented layouts and developed applications and websites using CakePHP, Joomla, and other related technologies.

Designed and developed a desktop and mobile application using Node-Webkit and Phonegap that automatically records web radio streams and shares them via the cloud. Project terminated due to legal cost issues.
The profession demanded creativity and I quite liked it, but unfortunately, I had to quit due to allergic reactions.

education.type: journeyman's certificate, specialized in Design and Technology
journeyman's certificate, early dropout due to allergic reactions.

interests.name: Reading
interests.summary: I'm absolutely hooked on classical, web, and light novels. They're my go-to for endless enjoyment and inspiration, keeping my love for literature alive and thriving.

interests.name: Gaming
interests.summary: I don't game as much as I used to, but when I do, I find myself gravitating towards what some might call "old man game´s" like Factorio, Satisfactory, simulators, and tycoon games. Interestingly, tycoon games were my favorite genre when I first started gaming.

interests.name: AI & Machine Learning
interests.summary: I thrive on building with AI, focusing heavily on local-first architecture and integrating open-source models directly into desktop applications. From experimenting with foundational LLMs to deploying lightweight models natively via ONNX for real-time classification, I’m all about breaking free from SaaS walled gardens. I might not be training massive clusters from scratch, but I'm masterfully orchestrating them right on the edge.

interests.name: Technology & Electronics
interests.summary: I love tinkering with electronics and writing low-level code for Arduinos and ESP microcontrollers. It's the physical hardware where my grand ideas usually meet reality. That said, my hands lack the steady precision required to be a soldering expert, meaning a career as a neurosurgeon is definitely off the table, and my desktop prototype wiring occasionally looks like a pirate's rigging.

interests.name: Anime
interests.summary: I've been a big anime fan since beeing a child, especially during my days of odd jobs with irregular schedules. It was a great way to unwind. However, it seems I'm showing my age, as I now find myself mostly revisiting old favorites or series I read the manga or novel of years ago.

interests.name: Manga
interests.summary: I was once deeply immersed in manga, but over time, I discovered that some of the series I loved had their origins in light novels. This revelation sparked a significant shift in my preference towards consuming novels.

interests.name: Gardening & Plants
interests.summary: I seem to prefer plants that serve a practical or edible purpose, though I'm not entirely sure why.

initiatives.name: Client Projects (NDA)
initiatives.summary: Ongoing full-stack development, API design, cloud infrastructure, and software consulting for various clients. Details protected by non-disclosure agreements.

initiatives.name: LLM Applications & AI Integration
initiatives.summary: Development of applications and workflows leveraging large language models including local LLM deployment (ollama, llama.cpp), Open WebUI, RAG pipelines, prompt engineering, and API wrappers for multiple AI providers.

initiatives.name: AI Image Generation Pipeline
initiatives.summary: Design and implementation of AI image generation pipelines using Stable Diffusion (ComfyUI, Automatic1111), custom LoRA training, batch generation and upscaling automation, and integration into existing workflows.

initiatives.name: Deta-PHP
initiatives.summary: PHP SDK for interacting with the Deta API. Provides a Deta client class for accessing Deta Base (NoSQL database) and Deta Drive (file storage).

initiatives.name: Deta-PHP-Engine
initiatives.summary:
PHP Runtime / Engine for Deta.Space. Based on PHP Version 8.1.8 using the Internal Webserver. Supports a wide range of PHP extensions including bcmath, curl, dom, gd, mbstring, mongodb, mysqlnd, pdo_mysql, pdo_sqlite, redis, simplexml, soap, sqlite3, swoole, tokenizer, xml, zip, and more.

Features:
- PHP 8.1.8
- Internal Webserver
- Support for multiple PHP Micros by duplicating the php folder and spacefile config
- Custom php.ini support via --with-config-file

initiatives.name: Deta-PHP-Sandbox
initiatives.summary:
Online Sandbox / Playground / Console to quickly test PHP code, deployed on Deta Space. Provides an interactive environment where users can paste PHP code, hit run, and see results in real-time without needing a local development setup.

Features syntax highlighting, code completion, and error handling. Accessible from anywhere, at any time, without additional software. Available at: https://deta.space/discovery/@tuefekci/phpsandbox

initiatives.name: deta-queue
initiatives.summary: Queue implementation for use with Deta Base, written in TypeScript. Provides a simple FIFO queue backed by Deta's NoSQL database with configurable TTL and queue name. Published as npm package `deta-queue`.

initiatives.name: deta-s3-server
initiatives.summary:
S3 Server for Deta.Space (Deta Drive) written in NodeJS based on jamhall/s3rver. A simple S3-compatible server that uses Deta Drive as a backend. Not a full S3 implementation — provides basic S3 API compatibility for storing and retrieving files via Deta Drive.

Available on Deta Space Discovery: https://deta.space/discovery/@tuefekci/s3server

initiatives.name: flatfilecache
initiatives.summary: Node.js flat file cache library written in TypeScript. Provides a simple file-based caching mechanism using the local filesystem, suitable for scenarios where a full database or Redis cache is not needed.

initiatives.name: novelsource-proxy
initiatives.summary: Proxy server for novel sources, complementing novelsourcePHP. Provides a proxy layer for web novel scraping operations, handling routing and requests between the scraping client and target novel sites.

initiatives.name: novelsourcePHP
initiatives.summary:
A collection of webnovel/novel sources offering varying amounts of scraping capability. Primarily built for meta data aggregation (tags and genres for recommendation) rather than content scraping.

Available as a Composer package: `tuefekci/novelsource`. Supports novel source adapters with search and last-checked tracking. Inspired by mensch272/novelsave_sources (Python) but built for PHP use cases.

initiatives.name: blkhole
initiatives.summary:
Blkhole serves as a download management tool designed for AllDebrid cloud downloads, facilitating the seamless automation of downloads directly to your local network.

Key features:
- User-friendly web interface for management of alldebrid.com downloads, cloud Torrent, Magnet, and DDL links
- Automated category-based downloading of completed cloud tasks to the local file system
- Download enhancements and optimizations
- Supports Magnet links, DDL links, Torrents, Chunked Downloads
- Bandwidth and connection limitation
- Automatic cleanup of finished and failed downloads
- Tested with Radarr and Sonarr

initiatives.name: docker-radarr-xu4
initiatives.summary: Docker configuration to run Radarr on an ODROID-XU4 single-board computer. Provides a containerized setup for the Radarr movie management tool optimized for ARM-based hardware.

initiatives.name: docker-sonarr-xu4
initiatives.summary: Docker configuration to run Sonarr on an ODROID-XU4 single-board computer. Includes a workaround to force the mono process onto one set of cores (big cores 4,5,6,7 and small cores 0,1,2,3) to resolve performance issues on the big.LITTLE ARM architecture.

initiatives.name: helpers
initiatives.summary:
Personal PHP helper library following PSR-4 autoloading and PSR-2 coding standards. Includes unit testing with PHPUnit 6, comprehensive guide and tutorial, and easy integration with any framework or plain PHP.

Requirements: PHP >= 7.0, Composer. Uses a standard project structure with build/, src/, tests/ directories, Travis CI configuration, and code sniffing tools (PHPCS, PHPCBF).

initiatives.name: overcli
initiatives.summary:
OverCLI is a PHP CLI / TUI Helper / Framework Library with a Render Loop, similar to how Game Engines handle application rendering. A collection of useful functions and classes for building CLI and TUI applications.

Features:
- Render Loop for continuous rendering with events
- Exception renderer for static error display
- Demo applications including a PIN IDENTIFICATION PROGRAM inspired by T2 Movie Prop
- Fully dependent on the Runtime, framerate is stable with fine memory usage

Requirements: PHP 7.2+ and Composer.

initiatives.name: php-benchmark
initiatives.summary:
Benchmarking PHP7, PHP8, and PHP7-parallel performance on an i7-4790k with 16GB DDR3. Tests include math operations, string manipulation, loops, and if/else conditions with 1,000,000 iterations across 16 workers.

Results (2021-10-04):
- PHP 7.4.24: Total 79.612s (serial)
- PHP 7.4.24-parallel: Total 9.361s (16 workers)
- PHP 8.0.11: Total 69.053s (serial)

Conclusion: PHP8 performs better than PHP7 especially in string manipulation, while parallel processing on PHP7 delivers the fastest results for suitable workloads.

initiatives.name: Android Web / Light Novel Reader
initiatives.summary: An Android app for reading web and light novels. It aggregates content from various sources and offers a customized reader mode with bookmarks, offline storage, and customizable display options.

initiatives.name: Download Manager (PHP → Svelte)
initiatives.summary: A cloud-based download manager migrated from a PHP backend to a modern Svelte/SvelteKit architecture. It manages and automates downloads from various cloud services with a reactive UI and WebSocket status updates.

initiatives.name: QR Code Redirect System
initiatives.summary: A powerful QR code redirect system built with the Slim Framework. It generates QR codes that dynamically redirect based on device type, location, or other parameters for marketing campaigns and dynamic print media links.

initiatives.name: ReactNative App und API (WebNovels)

initiatives.name: Retro Game Browser Emulator (WASM)
initiatives.summary: A platform-independent retro console emulator running in the browser with core logic ported to WebAssembly for native performance. Supports multiple classic consoles with save states and controller mapping.

initiatives.name: ReactNative CRM Task/Todo App
initiatives.summary: A ReactNative app for CRM software that forwards system-generated tasks to employees and reports issues back to the system.

initiatives.name: Reinigungsfirma CRM

initiatives.name: phpkg
initiatives.summary:
Package your PHP project into an executable for Linux, Mac and Windows. Provides source code protection by compiling PHP projects into standalone executables. Includes benchmark comparisons between the packaged (phpkg) version and native PHP 8.1.3 on an i5-8259U CPU.

Benchmark results showed minimal performance difference between phpkg and native PHP execution (64.334s vs 67.316s total for math, string, loop, and if/else tests), indicating the packaging adds negligible overhead.

initiatives.name: Facebook Events Automation API
initiatives.summary: An automation API for Facebook Events. The system automatically synchronizes Facebook events with a local database and enables further processing of event data in other systems — ideal for event calendars and ticketing platforms.

initiatives.name: Room Booking App (Android/iOS)
